channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: CI/CD pipeline abuse: the problem no one is watching
item.description: How we built an open-source, drop-in CI template that uses signal extraction and LLM reasoning to catch CI/CD abuse in GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Azure DevOps pipelines.

item.title: Vidar Rises to Top of Chaotic Infostealer Market
item.description: The malware has filled the gap created by last year's law enforcement takedowns of Lumma and Rhadamanthys.

item.title: Video service Vimeo confirms Anodot breach exposed user data
item.description: Vimeo has disclosed that data belonging to some of its customers and users has been accessed without authorization following the recent breach at the Anodot data anomaly detection company. [...]

item.title: What the March 2026 Threat Technique Catalog update means for your AWS environment
item.description: The AWS Customer Incident Response Team (AWS CIRT) regularly encounters patterns that repeat across their engagements when helping customers respond to security incidents. We’re passionate about making sure that infor...

item.title: Cyber Insurance Data Gives CISOs New Ammo for Budget Talks
item.description: Boards may ignore alerts, but they listen to losses: new data from Resilience links security gaps directly to financial impact. The post Cyber Insurance Data Gives CISOs New Ammo for Budget Talks appeared first on Sec...

item.title: Researchers Discover Critical GitHub CVE-2026-3854 RCE Flaw Exploitable via Single Git Push
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a critical security vulnerability impacting GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server that could allow an authenticated user to obtain remote code execution with a sin...

item.title: Brazilian LofyGang Resurfaces After Three Years With Minecraft LofyStealer Campaign
item.description: A cybercrime group of Brazilian origin has resurfaced after more than three years to orchestrate a campaign that targets Minecraft players with a new stealer called LofyStealer (aka GrabBot). "The malware disguises it...

item.title: Police arrest 10 suspected members of Black Axe cybercrime gang
item.description: A coordinated police operation in Switzerland has targeted suspected members of the Black Axe criminal network. On 28 April 2026, authorities carried out house searches across several Swiss cantons, leading to 10 arre...

item.title: Vimeo Confirms User and Customer Data Breach
item.description: The ShinyHunters group is threatening to leak stolen files unless Vimeo agrees to pay a ransom. The post Vimeo Confirms User and Customer Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: ShinyHunters claims it stole 1.4 million records from Udemy
item.description: The ShinyHunters group claims it has breached the Udemy, one of the world’s largest online learning platforms. According to Have I Been Pwned, the leaked dataset contained 1.4 million unique email addresses of custome...

item.title: Sevii unveils Cyber Swarm Defense Mode to stop AI-driven attacks at scale
item.description: Sevii has unveiled a new capability designed to stop high-volume, AI-powered cyberattacks at machine speed and scale, without the burden of unpredictable AI token costs. Sevii’s Cyber Swarm Defense Mode (CSD) addresse...

item.title: Alleged Chinese hacker extradited to US over cyberattacks targeting COVID-19 research
item.description: Chinese national Xu Zewei was extradited from Italy to the United States to face charges tied to an alleged cyber espionage campaign that breached thousands of computers worldwide. Xu is charged alongside Zhang Yu, wh...

item.title: Cequence Agent Personas bring granular control and governance to enterprise AI agents
item.description: Cequence Security has announced the general availability of Agent Personas in Cequence AI Gateway. These capabilities give enterprises granular, infrastructure-level control over what AI agents can do, down to individ...

item.title: NowSecure MARI gives enterprises evidence-based visibility into third-party mobile app risk
item.description: NowSecure has announced Mobile App Risk Intelligence (MARI), new capabilities that give enterprises evidence-based visibility into third-party mobile apps, as hidden AI features, opaque code, and unseen data flows cre...

item.title: The metrics killing your SOC, and what to use instead
item.description: Security operations centres risk being rendered entirely ineffective if organizations measure them using the wrong performance indicators, according to Dave Chismon, CTO for Architecture at UK’s National Cyber Securit...

item.title: US state privacy fines reached $3.425 billion in 2025
item.description: State privacy regulators across the United States collected $3.425 billion in privacy-related fines from companies in 2025. Gartner said the upward trend is expected to accelerate through 2028. Annual cumulative fines...

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2024-1708 ConnectWise ScreenConnect Path Traversal Vulnerability CVE-2026-322...

item.title: Canada’s first SMS blaster case leads to three arrests
item.description: Canadian law enforcement arrested three men who face 44 charges for operating an SMS blaster device that mimicked a legitimate cellular tower. The device was operated from vehicles, allowing it to move throughout the...

item.title: Linux storage management tool Stratis 3.9.0 adds online encryption and cache-less pool startup
item.description: Stratis is a tool for configuring pools and filesystems with enhanced storage functionality within the existing Linux storage management stack. It focuses on a command-line interface, an API, and an automated approach...

item.title: Access control with IAM Identity Center session tags
item.description: As organizations expand their Amazon Web Services (AWS) footprint, managing secure, scalable, and cost-efficient access across multiple accounts becomes increasingly important. AWS IAM Identity Center offers a central...

item.title: The Mythos Moment: Enterprises Must Fight Agents with Agents
item.description: Only with the right platform and an agentic, AI-driven defense, will enterprises be able to protect themselves in the agentic era. The post The Mythos Moment: Enterprises Must Fight Agents with Agents appeared first o...

item.title: Medtronic Confirms Data Breach After ShinyHunters Claims
item.description: Medtronic confirms IT breach as ShinyHunters claims millions of records accesseda

item.title: Securing GitHub: Wiz Research uncovers Remote Code Execution in GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server (CVE-2026-3854)
item.description: Details on CVE-2026-3854: A critical flaw in GitHub’s internal git infrastructure enabling RCE on GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server.

item.title: Webinar Today: A Step-by-Step Approach to AI Governance
item.description: Join the webinar to explore a practical, multi-layered roadmap to transition from fragmented AI usage to a governed, scalable ecosystem. The post Webinar Today: A Step-by-Step Approach to AI Governance appeared first...

item.title: Fresh Wave of GlassWorm VS Code Extensions Slices Through Supply Chain
item.description: Attackers continue to scale a campaign to seed Open VSX with seemingly benign VS Code extensions that spread self-propagating malware.

item.title: Checkmarx confirms LAPSUS$ hackers leaked its stolen GitHub data
item.description: Application security company Checkmarx has confirmed that the LAPSUS$ threat group leaked data stolen from its private GitHub repository. [...]

item.title: Robinhood Vulnerability Exploited for Phishing Attacks
item.description: Legitimate-looking emails coming from Robinhood systems lured recipients to phishing websites. The post Robinhood Vulnerability Exploited for Phishing Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: VECT 2.0 Ransomware Irreversibly Destroys Files Over 131KB on Windows, Linux, ESXi
item.description: Threat hunters are warning that the cybercriminal operation known as VECT 2.0 acts more like a wiper than a ransomware due to a critical flaw in its encryption implementation across Windows, Linux, and ESXi variants t...

item.title: Signal Phishing Campaign Targets German Officials in Suspected Russian Operation
item.description: Suspected Russian phishing via Signal targeted German officials, exploiting trust to access accounts and sensitive political communications. A new wave of cyber operations targeting European political leadership is on...

item.title: Get Motivated: What to Expect from Our Keynote at Rapid7's Global Cybersecurity Summit
item.description: Security teams prepare for incidents every day. Alerts are tuned, playbooks are built, and processes are tested. But when something actually happens, the challenge shifts. It becomes not just about making decisions un...

item.title: Alleged Chinese State Hacker Extradited to US
item.description: A member of Silk Typhoon, Xu Zewei is accused of launching cyberattacks against universities in the US. The post Alleged Chinese State Hacker Extradited to US appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Shutdowns, power outages, and conflict: a review of Q1 2026 Internet disruptions
item.description: The first quarter of 2026 saw a surge in Internet disruptions, from nationwide shutdowns in Uganda and Iran to unprecedented drone strikes on cloud infrastructure. We explore the data behind these events using Cloudfl...

item.title: Ransomware Turf War as 0APT and KryBit Groups Trade Blows
item.description: Ransomware groups 0APT and KryBit have doxxed each other online

item.title: The Bot Left a Fingerprint: Detecting and Attributing LLM-Generated Passwords
item.description: LLMs leave statistical fingerprints in the passwords they generate. We built a 100-year-old model to find them and detected 28,000 in the wild.

item.title: Inside an OPSEC Playbook: How Threat Actors Evade Detection
item.description: Threat actors are now publishing structured OPSEC playbooks to stay undetected. Flare reveals how these guides outline layered infrastructure, identity separation, and long-term evasion strategies. [...]

item.title: Dozens of Open VSX Extension Clones Linked to GlassWorm Malware
item.description: Over 70 cloned Open VSX extensions are likely sleeper extensions designed to distribute malware. The post Dozens of Open VSX Extension Clones Linked to GlassWorm Malware appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Sevii Launches Cyber Swarm Defense to Make Agentic AI Security Costs Predictable
item.description: Agentic AI can be expensive to use, causing further and unpredictable pressure on tight budgets. The post Sevii Launches Cyber Swarm Defense to Make Agentic AI Security Costs Predictable appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
item.description: Every security program is betting on the same assumption: once a system is connected, the problem is solved. Open a ticket, stand up a gateway, push the data through. Done. That assumption is wrong. It is also a major...

item.title: Electric Motorcycles and Scooters Face Hacking Risks to Security and Rider Safety
item.description: Vulnerabilities in Zero Motorcycles electric motorcycles and Yadea electric scooters can pose physical security and safety risks. The post Electric Motorcycles and Scooters Face Hacking Risks to Security and Rider Saf...

item.title: No Patch for New PhantomRPC Privilege Escalation Technique in Windows
item.description: A fake RPC server can be used to listen for RPC requests and impersonate the target service to elevate privileges to System. The post No Patch for New PhantomRPC Privilege Escalation Technique in Windows appeared firs...

item.title: Microsoft fixes Entra ID flaw enabling privilege escalation
item.description: Microsoft fixed a Microsoft Entra ID flaw where the Agent ID Administrator role could enable privilege escalation and account takeover. Microsoft addressed a flaw in Microsoft Entra ID that could let attackers take ov...

item.title: Critical Unpatched Flaw Leaves Hugging Face LeRobot Open to Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a critical security flaw impacting LeRobot, Hugging Face's open-source robotics platform with nearly 24,000 GitHub stars, that could be exploited to achieve remote c...

item.title: What Anthropic’s Mythos Means for the Future of Cybersecurity
item.description: Two weeks ago, Anthropic announced that its new model, Claude Mythos Preview, can autonomously find and weaponize software vulnerabilities, turning them into working exploits without expert guidance. These were vulner...

item.title: Germany Suspects Russia Is Behind Signal Phishing That Targeted Top Officials
item.description: Federal prosecutors have been conducting a preliminary investigation since mid-February 2026 into alleged cyberattacks on Signal accounts. The post Germany Suspects Russia Is Behind Signal Phishing That Targeted Top O...

item.title: After Mythos: New Playbooks For a Zero-Window Era
item.description: When patching isn’t fast enough, NDR helps contain the next era of threats. If you’ve been tracking advancements in AI, you know the exploit window, the short buffer that organizations relied on to patch and protect a...

item.title: Microsoft: New Remote Desktop warnings may display incorrectly
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed a new issue causing newly introduced Windows security warnings to display incorrectly when opening Remote Desktop (.rdp) files. [...]

item.title: Microsoft asks iPhone users to reauthenticate after Outlook outage
item.description: After addressing a widespread outage that affected Outlook.com users worldwide on Monday, Microsoft has asked iPhone users to re-enter their credentials to regain access to their Outlook and Hotmail accounts via the d...

item.title: Chinese Silk Typhoon Hacker Extradited to U.S. Over COVID Research Cyberattacks
item.description: A Chinese national accused of being a member of the Silk Typhoon hacking group has been extradited to the U.S. from Italy. Xu Zewei, 34, was arrested in July 2025 by Italian authorities for his alleged links to the Ch...

item.title: No Metrics Are Better Than Bad Metrics in the SOC, Says NCSC
item.description: The National Cyber Security Centre has warned against measuring SOCs with ticket-based metrics

item.title: Spectrum Security Emerges From Stealth Mode With $19 Million
item.description: The threat detection startup will invest in accelerating its engineering and go-to-market efforts. The post Spectrum Security Emerges From Stealth Mode With $19 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: North Korean Hackers Target Crypto Firms with ClickFix and AI-Made Zoom Lures
item.description: Arctic Wolf attributed this large-scale spear-phishing campaign to BlueNoroff, a financially motivated subgroup of the Lazarus Group

item.title: MDR Selection is a Partnership Decision
item.description: Managed Detection and Response (MDR) is a cybersecurity service that combines human expertise and technology to detect, investigate, and respond to threats 24/7. I write this as a Field CISO at Rapid7, but also as som...

item.title: NCSC launches SilentGlass, a plug-in device to secure HDMI and DisplayPort links
item.description: NCSC’s SilentGlass blocks malicious HDMI/DisplayPort links, protecting monitors from hardware attacks. Now commercialized for global use. The UK’s National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) has launched SilentGlass, a new...

item.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
item.description: An administrative role meant for artificial intelligence (AI) agents within Microsoft Entra ID could enable privilege escalation and identity takeover attacks, according to new findings from Silverfort. Agent ID Admin...

item.title: Medtronic Hack Confirmed After ShinyHunters Threatens Data Leak
item.description: The ShinyHunters cybercrime group claimed to have stolen 9 million records containing personal information from Medtronic. The post Medtronic Hack Confirmed After ShinyHunters Threatens Data Leak appeared first on Sec...

item.title: Microsoft Confirms Active Exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202
item.description: Microsoft on Monday revised its advisory for a now-patched, high-severity security flaw impacting Windows Shell to acknowledge that it has been actively exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026...

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, April 28th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9908, (Tue, Apr 28th)
Going beyond CVEs: Chainguard’s one day KEV SLA
item.description: Chainguard introduces a 1-day KEV SLA, ensuring exploited vulnerabilities are fixed fast—aligned with how security teams prioritize real-world threats.

item.title: CrowdStrike Named a Leader in Frost & Sullivan 2026 Radar for Cloud-Native Application Protection Platforms
Malicious Release of elementary-data PyPI Package Steals Cloud Credentials from Data Engineers
item.description: Attackers exploited a GitHub Actions script injection vulnerability to publish a malicious version of the elementary-data Python CLI (v0.23.3), embedding a credential-stealing backdoor that targeted dbt profiles, clou...

Robinhood account creation flaw abused to send phishing emails
item.description: Online trading platform Robinhood's account creation process was exploited by threat actors to inject phishing messages into legitimate emails, tricking users into believing their accounts had suspicious activity. [...]

GlassWorm malware attacks return via 73 OpenVSX "sleeper" extensions
item.description: A new wave of the Glassworm campaign is targeting the OpenVSX ecosystem with 73 "sleeper" extensions that turn malicious after an update. [...]

item.title: UNC6692 Combines Social Engineering, Malware, Cloud Abuse
item.description: A newly discovered threat actor is using Microsoft Teams, AWS S3 buckets, and custom "Snow" malware in a multipronged campaign.

item.title: Canada arrests three for operating “SMS blaster” device in Toronto
item.description: Canadian authorities have arrested three men for operating an "SMS blaster" device that pretends to be a cellular tower to send phishing texts to nearby phones. [...]

item.title: Medtronic discloses security incident after ShinyHunters claimed theft of 9M+ records
item.description: Medtronic confirmed a breach of its IT systems after ShinyHunters claimed the theft of over 9 million records. Medtronic confirmed a cyberattack on its corporate IT systems after the hacker group ShinyHunters claimed...

item.title: FTC: Americans lost over $2.1 billion to social media scams in 2025
item.description: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) warned of a massive increase in losses from social media scams since 2020, exceeding $2.1 billion in 2025. [...]

item.description: High Vulnerabilities Primary Vendor -- Product Description Published CVSS Score Source Info Patch Info Thinkphp--ThinkPHP ThinkPHP 5.0.23 contains a remote code execution vulnerability that allows unauthenticated atta...

item.title: Can I do that with policy? Understanding the AWS Service Authorization Reference
item.description: Understanding what AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies can control helps you build better security controls and avoid spending time on approaches that won’t work. You’ve likely encountered questions like...

item.title: Unpatched 'PhantomRPC' Flaw in Windows Enables Privilege Escalation
item.description: A researcher discovered five different exploit paths that stem from an architectural weakness in how Windows' Remote Procedure Call (RPC) mechanism handles connections to unavailable services.

item.title: PyPI package with 1.1M monthly downloads hacked to push infostealer
item.description: An attacker pushed a malicious version of the popular elementary-data package Python Package Index (PyPI) to steal sensitive developer data and cryptocurrency wallets. [...]

item.title: US Sanctions Target Cambodian Scam Network Leaders
item.description: US sanctions target Cambodian scam networks tied to crypto fraud and trafficking

item.title: Home security giant ADT data breach affects 5.5 million people
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion group stole the personal information of 5.5 million individuals after breaching the systems of home security giant ADT earlier this month, according to data breach notification service Have...

item.description: On Thursday, April 30 at 2:00 PM ET, BleepingComputer will host a live webinar with threat intelligence company Flare and threat intelligence researcher Tammy Harper, exploring how security teams can identify early wa...

item.title: Checkmarx Confirms GitHub Repository Data Posted on Dark Web After March 23 Attack
item.description: Checkmarx has disclosed that its ongoing investigation tied to the supply chain security incident has revealed that a cybercriminal group published data related to the company on the dark web. "Based on current eviden...

item.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 008 - 26-Day Pause Ends with Three Concurrent Compromises (Checkmarx KICS, Bitwarden CLI Cascade, xinference PyPI), CanisterSprawl npm Worm Identified, and Tier 1 Coverage Returns, (Mon, Apr 27th)
item.description: This update succeeds&#;x26;#;xc2;&#;x26;#;xa0;TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign Update 007, published April 8, 2026, which left the campaign in credential-monetization mode following the Cisco source code theft via Trivy-...

item.title: Utilities Tech Supplier Itron Discloses Cyber-Attack, Operations Unaffected
item.description: Itron confirmed a cyber incident but does not believe it is likely to have a material impact on the company

item.title: Medtronic confirms breach after hackers claim 9 million records theft
item.description: Medical device giant Medtronic disclosed last week that hackers breached its network and accessed data in "certain corporate IT systems." [...]

item.title: Chinese spy posed as researcher in spear-phishing campaign targeting NASA to steal defense software
item.description: A Chinese national posed as a U.S. researcher, tricking NASA staff in a phishing campaign to steal sensitive data tied to defense software and exports. A Chinese national ran a spear-phishing campaign by posing as a U...

item.title: LINKEDIN BROWSERGATE
item.description: BrowserGate claims LinkedIn secretly fingerprints users via extensions and device data, sending encrypted results to third parties for tracking. BrowserGate is an investigation conducted by Fairlinked (https://browser...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fast16 Malware, XChat Launch, Federal Backdoor, AI Employee Tracking & More
item.description: Everything is dumb again. This week feels broken in a very familiar way. Old tricks are back. New tools are doing shady crap. Supply chains got hit. Fake help desks worked. Weird research showed how easy some attacks...

item.title: Short-Lived Credentials in Agentic Systems: A Practical Trade-off Guide
item.description: Understand where short-lived credentials reduce risk in agentic systems and where operational complexity requires stronger monitoring and governance controls.

item.title: 20-Year-Old Malware Rewrites History of Cyber Sabotage
item.description: Researchers have uncovered a malware framework dubbed "fast16" that predates Stuxnet by 5 years.

item.title: Incomplete Windows Patch Opens Door to Zero-Click Attacks
item.description: The initial vulnerability was exploited by Russia-linked APT28 in attacks against Ukraine and EU countries. The post Incomplete Windows Patch Opens Door to Zero-Click Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Deepfake Voice Attacks are Outpacing Defenses: What Security Leaders Should Know
item.description: Three seconds of audio is all it takes to clone a voice for fraud. Adaptive Security shows how deepfake calls trick employees into sending real money—and why most defenses don't catch them. [...]

item.title: Parsing Agentic Offensive Security's Existential Threat
item.description: Some fear frontier LLMs like Claude Mythos and Anthropic's GPT-5.5 will lead to cybersecurity annihilation. Ari Herbert-Voss notes this could be an opportunity.

item.title: OpenSSH Flaw Allowing Full Root Shell Access Lurked for 15 Years
item.description: A code reuse issue enabled comma characters in certificate principals to be interpreted as list separators. The post OpenSSH Flaw Allowing Full Root Shell Access Lurked for 15 Years appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Malicious AI Prompt Injection Attacks Increasing, but Sophistication Still Low: Google
item.description: The tech giant found that many indirect prompt injection attempts are harmless, but some malicious exploits have also been identified. The post Malicious AI Prompt Injection Attacks Increasing, but Sophistication Stil...

item.title: Mythos Changed the Math on Vulnerability Discovery. Most Teams Aren't Ready for the Remediation Side
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude Mythos Preview has dominated security discussions since its April 7 announcement. Early reporting describes a powerful cybersecurity-focused AI system capable of identifying vulnerabilities at scale...

item.title: PhantomCore Exploits TrueConf Vulnerabilities to Breach Russian Networks
item.description: A pro-Ukrainian hacktivist group called PhantomCore has been attributed to attacks actively targeting servers running TrueConf video conferencing software in Russia since September 2025. That's according to a report p...

item.title: Most Cybersecurity Professionals Feel Undervalued and Underpaid
item.description: A new report by global technology recruitment firm, Harvey Nash, found that three quarters of cybersecurity staff are pessimistic on pay and half are looking for a new job

item.title: Researchers Uncover 73 Fake VS Code Extensions Delivering GlassWorm v2 Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged dozens of Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extensions on the Open VSX repository that are linked to a persistent information-stealing campaign dubbed GlassWorm. The cluster...

item.title: Energy and Water Management Firm Itron Hacked
item.description: Itron, which serves utilities and cities around the world, discovered unauthorized access to its systems on April 13. The post Energy and Water Management Firm Itron Hacked appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: NIST NVD Update: What it Means For Vulnerability Management
item.description: The shift from static CVE scoring to risk-based prioritization signals a new era for Vulnerability Managers

item.description: New Orleans, LA — The OWASP Foundation is pleased to announce the appointment of Missie Lindsey as Director of Corporate Relations. Missie brings more than 18 years of experience in B2B marketing, corporate partnershi...

item.title: Firefox bug CVE-2026-6770 enabled cross-site tracking and Tor fingerprinting
item.description: CVE-2026-6770 let attackers fingerprint Firefox and Tor users, even in Private mode. Firefox 150 and Tor Browser 15.0.10 fixed it. A vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-6770, allowed attackers to fingerprint Firefox us...

item.title: UNC6692 Uses Email Bombing, Social Engineering to Deploy ‘Snow’ Malware
item.description: The threat actor infected victims with the Snow malware family – Snowbelt, Snowglaze, and Snowbasin – for persistent access. The post UNC6692 Uses Email Bombing, Social Engineering to Deploy ‘Snow’ Malware appeared fi...

item.title: Easily Exploitable ‘Pack2TheRoot’ Linux Vulnerability Leads to Root Access
item.description: A race condition in PackageKit allows unprivileged users to escalate privileges when installing packages. The post Easily Exploitable ‘Pack2TheRoot’ Linux Vulnerability Leads to Root Access appeared first on SecurityW...

item.title: US Launches Sweeping Crackdown on Southeast Asia Cyberscams and Sanctions Cambodian Senator
item.description: US conducts sweeping crackdown on Southeast Asian cyberscam operations as part of what officials say is a “new theater of war”. The post US Launches Sweeping Crackdown on Southeast Asia Cyberscams and Sanctions Cambod...

item.title: Researchers Identify Fast16 Sabotage Malware That Pre-Dates Stuxnet
item.description: The “fast16” malware may have been used to target Iran’s nuclear program prior to Stuxnet

item.title: Fast16: Pre-Stuxnet malware that targeted precision engineering software
item.description: Fast16 is a pre-Stuxnet malware that tampered with precision software and spread itself. Evidence suggests links to U.S. operations during early cyber tensions. SentinelOne uncovered Fast16, a sabotage malware used in...

item.title: Firefox Vulnerability Allows Tor User Fingerprinting
item.description: The vulnerability is tracked as CVE-2026-6770 and it has been patched with the release of Firefox 150 and Tor 15.0.10. The post Firefox Vulnerability Allows Tor User Fingerprinting appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Italy moves to extradite Chinese national to the U.S. over hacking charges
item.description: Italy plans to extradite Xu Zewei to the U.S. over alleged hacks on COVID-19 research tied to state-backed operations. Italy is moving to extradite Xu Zewei, the Chinese national arrested in 2025 at the request of U.S...

item.title: Fake CAPTCHA IRSF Scam and 120 Keitaro Campaigns Drive Global SMS, Crypto Fraud
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a telecommunications fraud campaign that uses fake CAPTCHA verification tricks to dupe unsuspecting users into sending international text messages that incur charges...

item.title: BlackFile Group Targets Retail and Hospitality with Vishing Attacks
item.description: Researchers uncover a new data theft and extortion group dubbed “BlackFile”

item.title: Your IAM was built for humans, AI agents don’t care
item.description: Identity and access management was built for a simpler world. One where the hardest problem was a human logging in, and where “Who are you?” was sufficient to decide what someone could do. That model served enterprise...

item.title: U.S. utility giant Itron discloses a security breach
item.description: Itron detected unauthorized access to part of its IT environment on April 13, 2026, and launched incident response and notified authorities. Itron disclosed a cyber incident involving unauthorized access to part of it...

item.title: The AI criminal mastermind is already hiring on gig platforms
item.description: Labor-hire platforms let anyone with a credit card post a task and pay a stranger to complete it. The RentAHuman platform extends that model to AI agents through a Model Context Protocol server, allowing an agent to p...

item.title: 25 open-source cybersecurity tools that don’t care about your budget
item.description: Regardless of the operating system you use, managing secrets, apps, cloud, compliance, and security operations can be overwhelming. The free, open-source tools presented in this article can help you detect threats, in...

item.title: Product showcase: LuLu reveals unauthorized outbound connections from Mac apps
item.description: LuLu is a free, open-source firewall for macOS that lets you control which apps are allowed to send data from your computer. macOS includes a built-in firewall, but it mainly handles incoming connections. LuLu also mo...

item.title: Mythos pulls zero-days forward. Here's what you need to know now.
item.description: Anthropic's Mythos is reshaping zero-day threats. Learn how Chainguard helps you stay ahead with source-built, continuously secure software supply chains.

item.title: CVE-2026-42208: Targeted SQL injection against LiteLLM's authentication path discovered 36 hours following vulnerability disclosure
Qinglong task scheduler RCE vulnerabilities exploited in the wild for cryptomining
item.description: Two authentication bypass vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-3965, CVE-2026-4047) in the Qinglong task scheduling panel were exploited in the wild to deploy cryptomining malware. Here's what happened, how the attacks worked, a...

item.title: Introducing the New Agentic Architecture for Snyk Agent Fix: Faster, Smarter, and More Secure
item.description: Snyk Agent Fix upgrades to a new agentic architecture for faster, smarter, and more secure AI-powered code fixes. Now with full Snyk Code language coverage and verified remediation.
